input: Design the target architecture for migrating our banking monolith to microservices
expected_behavior: Skill activates and produces C4 L2 container diagram, 6+ ADRs (saga, ACL, zero trust, event sourcing, K8s, caching), nightmare scenarios with mitigations, MVP component, and Strangler Fig migration plan

input: Create a TO-BE architecture for a conservative modernization — max 3 services, minimal risk
expected_behavior: Skill activates with conservative conditional logic: monolith-first approach, 2-3 services max, 10-20% replacement over 18 months, ADRs emphasizing stability and risk mitigation

input: Design target architecture but we cannot use any cloud services — everything must be on-premises
expected_behavior: Skill activates, replaces K8s with OpenShift or VM-based deployment, replaces managed services with self-hosted equivalents, documents increased operational overhead in ADRs

input: Mitigate nightmare scenarios for our new distributed payment system
expected_behavior: Skill activates and produces 5+ nightmare scenarios (ghost transaction, cascade failure, auth unavailable, schema drift, legacy corruption) with defense-in-depth mitigations, monitoring alerts, and acceptance criteria

input: Analyze our current codebase and assess the existing architecture quality
expected_behavior: Skill should NOT activate. This is a current-state (AS-IS) analysis request, not target-state design. Should route to asis-analysis skill instead.
